nameMeanings.name: Poole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English pol(e), polle, poul(e) ‘pool, pond’ (Old English pōl). The name may be topographic, for someone who lived by a pool, or habitative, from a place so named.
Possibly an Americanized form of German Puhl or Pfuhl(e) (see Pool 4).

variants.name: Pool
meanings: English: variant of   Poole   or   Paul  .
Jewish (from the Netherlands) and Dutch: ethnic name for someone from Poland.

variants.name: Pooler
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire): topographic name for someone who lived near a pool, or a habitational name for someone from a place called with this word (see   Poole  ).
Americanized form of French   Poulin   ‘young animal, colt’.
In some cases also an Americanized form of German Pfuhler, a variant of Pfuhl + -er, suffix denoting an inhabitant.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Molde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Molde, derived from a word meaning ‘soil rich in humus, topsoil’.
North German: metonymic occupational name from Middle Low German molde ‘oblong wooden bowl (used for baking etc.)’.
English: variant of   Mould  . This surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Mould
meanings: from the Middle English female personal names Mold, Moud, or Molt, shortened forms of Anglo-Norman French Mahold, a common variant of Mahald; see Maude 2.

nameMeanings.name: Maude
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Maud  , the usual spelling of the name in North America.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Molde Maude Poole was born in 1436, in Middlesex, England, her father, John de Poole, was 16 and her mother, Elizabeth Poole, was 15. She married Hugh John Tuttle in 1455. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1510, in England, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Molde Maude Poole est née en 1436, à Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, John de Poole, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Poole, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh John Tuttle en 1455. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1510, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Molde Maude Poole 1436 in Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Poole 16 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Poole 15. Sie hat 1455 Hugh John Tuttle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1510 im Alter von 74 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Molde Maude Poole è nata il nel 1436 nel Middlesex, England, sua padre, John de Poole, aveva 16 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Poole, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Hugh John Tuttle nel 1455. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1510, nel England, all'età di 74 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Molde Maude Poole(이)가 1436년 영국 미들레세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Poole(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Poole(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1455년 Hugh John Tuttle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1510년 영국에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Molde Maude Poole родилась в 1436 г. в местности миддлесекс, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Poole было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Poole, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh John Tuttle в 1455 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1510 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Molde Maude Poole nació en 1436, en Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Poole, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Poole, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Hugh John Tuttle en 1455. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1510, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 74 años.
